name: How do I know if a handyman can legally do my repair?

mainEntity.name: What jobs are small enough for a handyman, and what jobs need a licensed trade?
mainEntity.text: What jobs are small enough for a handyman, and what jobs need a licensed trade?

acceptedAnswer.text: The easiest way to decide is to look at the scale and risk of the job. A handyman is usually appropriate for minor repairs, cosmetic work, assembly, mounting, caulking, small carpentry fixes, and basic maintenance. If the work involves structural changes, major plumbing, electrical upgrades, gas lines, or anything that requires permits or inspections, a licensed specialist is often the safer choice. In practice, many homeowners are unsure where the line falls, especially when a small visible issue may hide a bigger problem behind it. The most useful service pages explain common examples of handyman-appropriate tasks and then list the types of jobs that should be reviewed more carefully. For homeowners in coastal San Mateo County, that distinction matters because weather damage can make a simple exterior repair turn into a more complex construction issue. A quick photo-based estimate or walkthrough can help identify whether the repair stays small or needs a different trade.
acceptedAnswer.description: A handyman is usually the right choice for minor repairs, cosmetic fixes, assembly, mounting, and small maintenance tasks. Jobs that involve major plumbing, electrical, structural changes, gas lines, or permitted construction often need a licensed specialist. When in doubt, ask for a scope review before booking.
